Federal Signal ( (FSS) ) has shared an announcement.

At its April 21, 2026 annual meeting, Federal Signal reported that holders of 57,977,820 shares, or 95.2% of outstanding common stock as of the February 23 record date, were represented in person or by proxy, establishing a quorum. Stockholders elected eight directors, including CEO Jennifer L. Sherman, to one-year terms, with vote totals indicating solid support for the slate despite some withhold votes for certain directors.

Investors also approved, on an advisory basis, the company’s 2026 named executive officer compensation, signaling general endorsement of Federal Signal’s pay practices, though more than 4.3 million shares were voted against. In addition, stockholders ratified Deloitte & Touche LLP as the independent registered public accounting firm for 2026, reinforcing continuity in the company’s audit and financial reporting oversight framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (FSS) stock is a Buy with a $130.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Federal Signal stock, see the FSS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FSS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FSS is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (growth, margins, and free cash flow) and an upbeat earnings-call outlook with robust 2026 guidance. The main offsets are a relatively expensive valuation (high P/E and low yield) and only moderate technical momentum despite an underlying uptrend.

To see Spark’s full report on FSS stock, click here.

More about Federal Signal

Federal Signal Corporation operates in the industrial and safety equipment sector, with a focus on products and services that support municipal, commercial, and industrial customers. The company’s operations are overseen by a board of directors elected annually by stockholders, reflecting a typical U.S. public company governance structure aimed at aligning management with shareholder interests.

data.title: FS Bancorp Posts Q1 Earnings, Declares Quarterly Dividend
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

FS Bancorp ( (FSBW) ) has issued an update.

FS Bancorp, Inc., the parent of 1st Security Bank of Washington, provides a mix of commercial, consumer and real estate lending, deposit products, cash management services and home mortgage origination across its Commercial and Consumer Banking and Home Lending segments. The bank maintains a sizable loan portfolio in commercial real estate and consumer lending, and continues to build out its geographic reach, including projected expansion into the Portland, Oregon market.

On April 21, 2026, FS Bancorp reported first-quarter 2026 net income of $7.8 million, or $1.02 per diluted share, slightly below both the prior quarter and the year-ago period, though pre-tax income rose 4.6% year-over-year to $9.9 million. The board declared its 53rd consecutive quarterly cash dividend of $0.29 per share, supported by stable core deposits of $2.31 billion, 4.9% annual loan growth, a record split-adjusted book value per share of $42.42, stronger Home Lending production and solid regulatory capital ratios, while share repurchases underscored ongoing capital return to investors.

Management highlighted the announced February merger with Pacific West Bancorp and anticipated 2026 entry into the Portland, Oregon market as strategic growth drivers. Segment results showed $6.7 million in net income from Commercial and Consumer Banking and $1.1 million from Home Lending, with improved mortgage activity and disciplined deposit pricing contributing to earnings resilience despite a higher effective tax rate and softness in consumer loan balances.

The most recent analyst rating on (FSBW) stock is a Hold with a $46.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on FS Bancorp stock, see the FSBW Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on FSBW Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, FSBW is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by 2025 fundamental weakness (sharp revenue decline and a major drop in operating/free cash flow) and bearish technicals (below key moving averages with negative MACD). Offsetting factors include attractive valuation (low P/E and solid dividend yield) and generally positive corporate developments (accretive M&A and a dividend increase).

To see Spark’s full report on FSBW stock, click here.

More about FS Bancorp

FS Bancorp, Inc., the holding company for 1st Security Bank of Washington, operates through Commercial and Consumer Banking and Home Lending segments. It offers deposit products, residential, consumer, business and commercial real estate lending, cash management services, and originates one-to-four-family residential mortgages, with a growing focus on markets including the Portland, Oregon area.

The Commercial and Consumer Banking segment manages the bank’s investment portfolio and other assets, while the Home Lending segment primarily sells mortgage loans into secondary markets. Together these operations support a loan book centered on commercial real estate and high-credit-quality consumer home improvement lending, underpinned by solid regulatory capital ratios.

Average Trading Volume: 11,261

Technical Sentiment Signal: Strong Buy

Current Market Cap: $313M

data.title: Greene County Bancorp Maintains Quarterly Dividend, MHC Waives Payout
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Greene County Bancorp ( (GCBC) ) has provided an update.

On April 22, 2026, Greene County Bancorp, Inc. announced that its board approved a quarterly cash dividend of $0.10 per share on its common stock for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, maintaining an annualized rate of $0.40 per share, unchanged from the prior quarter. The dividend will be paid on May 29, 2026, to shareholders of record as of May 15, 2026, signaling continued capital returns and stability in the company’s payout policy.

Greene County Bancorp, MHC, the federal mutual holding company that owns 54.1% of the outstanding common shares, will waive its right to receive this dividend, following Federal Reserve nonobjection to waive dividends of up to $0.64 per share across four quarters in 2026. This waiver concentrates the cash distribution among public minority shareholders while allowing the company to preserve capital at the majority-owner level, which may support balance-sheet flexibility and stakeholder confidence in the community bank’s financial management.

Spark’s Take on GCBC Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, GCBC is a Outperform.

The score is driven primarily by strong financial performance (healthy profitability and cash conversion despite some margin compression) and an attractive valuation (low P/E with a modest yield). Technicals are moderately positive with price above key moving averages and supportive momentum, while recent corporate actions (buyback authorization and dividend waiver approval) add a favorable shareholder-return tailwind.

To see Spark’s full report on GCBC stock, click here.

More about Greene County Bancorp

Greene County Bancorp, Inc., the holding company for the Bank of Greene County and Greene County Commercial Bank, is a leading provider of community-based banking services in New York’s Hudson Valley and Capital Region. The Nasdaq-listed company serves individuals, businesses, municipalities and other institutions, with a focus on supporting local economic development and community quality of life.

The company’s operations center on traditional banking products such as deposit accounts, loans and related financial services tailored to regional customers. By emphasizing its community-bank model and local relationships, Greene County Bancorp positions itself as a key financial intermediary for smaller markets outside major metropolitan centers in upstate New York.

data.title: Maison Solutions Enacts Reverse Stock Split to Retain Nasdaq Listing
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Maison Solutions Inc. Class A ( (MSS) ) has provided an announcement.

Maison Solutions Inc., a U.S.-based specialty grocery retailer offering traditional Asian food and merchandise to Asian-American communities, operates supermarkets under the HK Good Fortune banner in Los Angeles and Lee Lee International Supermarket in the Phoenix and Tucson metro areas. Since its 2019 formation, the company has focused on providing culturally attuned fresh food and daily necessities to diverse neighborhoods in major Western U.S. markets.

On April 13, 2026, Maison Solutions’ board approved a 1-for-10 reverse stock split of its Class A common stock, which became effective at 12:01 a.m. Eastern Time on April 24, 2026 and was announced publicly on April 22, 2026. The move, previously authorized by a stockholder vote on October 19, 2025, reduced outstanding shares from about 28.8 million to roughly 2.9 million, is aimed at lifting the share price to meet Nasdaq’s $1.00 minimum bid requirement, and leaves ownership percentages, authorized share counts and par values for common and preferred stock essentially unchanged.

Following the reverse split, Maison Solutions’ stock continues to trade on the Nasdaq Capital Market under the symbol MSS, now under a new CUSIP, with fractional shares rounded up to the nearest whole share to avoid cash-in-lieu payments. The consolidation also triggered proportional adjustments to outstanding warrants, convertible securities and stock incentive plans, a step designed to preserve investor economic positions while supporting the company’s effort to maintain its Nasdaq listing and access to public capital markets.

The most recent analyst rating on (MSS) stock is a Buy with a $4.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Maison Solutions Inc. Class A stock, see the MSS Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on MSS Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, MSS is a Neutral.

The score is weighed down primarily by weak financial performance (losses, negative cash flow, and high leverage) and a clearly bearish technical backdrop (below all major moving averages with negative MACD). Valuation provides limited support due to negative earnings and no dividend, while the Nasdaq compliance event adds additional downside risk.

To see Spark’s full report on MSS stock, click here.

More about Maison Solutions Inc. Class A

Maison Solutions Inc. is a U.S.-based specialty grocery retailer focused on traditional Asian food and merchandise, serving primarily Asian-American communities. Founded in 2019, the company holds equity interests in supermarkets operating as HK Good Fortune in the Los Angeles area and Lee Lee International Supermarket in the Phoenix and Tucson, Arizona markets.

Its stores emphasize Asian fresh produce, meat, seafood and daily necessities tailored to traditional family values while adapting to younger, faster-paced lifestyles. Maison Solutions aims to meet the needs of culturally diverse neighborhoods where it operates, positioning itself as a niche player in the growing ethnic and specialty grocery segment.

Average Trading Volume: 1,557,724

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: $4.46M

Learn more about MSS stock on TipRanks’ Stock Analysis page.

data.title: UBS Warns Swiss Capital Overhaul Could Force USD 37 Billion in Extra CET1 After Credit Suisse Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B ( (AMUB) ) has provided an announcement.

On April 22, 2026, UBS sharply criticized new Swiss regulatory capital measures, arguing that the finalized Capital Adequacy Ordinance and proposed Banking Act changes are extreme, out of line with international standards and potentially damaging to the Swiss economy. The bank said it is still reviewing the government’s materials and will give further detail with its first‑quarter 2026 results on April 29.

Under the new ordinance, UBS’s capitalized software will be amortized over a maximum of three years for capital purposes and prudential valuation adjustments will rise, changes that together are expected to remove about USD 4 billion of net CET1 capital at group level and USD 2 billion at UBS AG, cutting the group CET1 ratio by roughly 0.8 percentage points once fully implemented between 2027 and 2029. Separately, the proposal to fully deduct foreign participations from UBS AG’s standalone CET1, phased in over seven years, would force the bank to hold around USD 20 billion in additional CET1 capital, lifting the de facto minimum CET1 ratio at group level to about 18.4% and, after CAO‑related derecognition, to roughly 17.6%.

UBS said these new requirements would come on top of around USD 15 billion of extra CET1 capital already needed because of the Credit Suisse takeover, taking total additional CET1 demands to about USD 37 billion and implying annual capital costs of some USD 3 billion. The bank warned this would further understate its capital strength versus peers and cited an independent BAK Economics study suggesting the proposed full deduction of foreign participations alone could cost Switzerland up to CHF 34 billion in cumulative GDP over ten years, alongside lower investment, employment and tax revenues.

Despite the looming changes, UBS is maintaining its targets of an underlying 15% return on CET1 capital and a sub‑70% cost/income ratio by end‑2026, and it reaffirmed its planned 2026 capital returns. The group said it will continue to support fact‑based parliamentary deliberations, seek ways to protect shareholders while limiting any impact on clients and employees, and press for recognition of the economic and competitive risks posed by further tightening an already tough Swiss capital framework.

More about ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B

UBS Group AG, headquartered in Zurich and Basel, is Switzerland’s largest global wealth manager and a leading domestic bank, with complementary investment banking and asset management operations. The group, which recently absorbed Credit Suisse, operates under one of the world’s most stringent capital regimes and plays a central role in Switzerland’s financial system and broader economy.

Average Trading Volume: 612

data.title: UBS Warns Swiss Capital Plan Could Add $37 Billion Burden After Credit Suisse Deal
data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B ( (AMUB) ) has issued an announcement.

On 22 April 2026, UBS sharply criticized new Swiss regulatory capital measures after the Federal Council finalized changes to the Capital Adequacy Ordinance and sent proposed Banking Act amendments on foreign participations to parliament, arguing the package is extreme, not internationally aligned and potentially damaging to the Swiss economy. The revised rules will tighten capital treatment for capitalized software and prudential valuation adjustments from 2027–2029, cutting about USD 4 billion of CET1 at group level and USD 2 billion at UBS AG standalone, while a separate proposal to fully deduct foreign participations from CET1 over seven years would force UBS AG to hold roughly USD 20 billion in additional CET1, lifting total extra capital needs linked to the Credit Suisse deal to about USD 37 billion and driving annual capital costs of around USD 3 billion.

UBS said the Swiss government’s pro‑forma capital ratios and peer comparisons are misleading and warned that the combined impact of the measures would push its de facto minimum group CET1 ratio to about 18.4%, or 17.6% after CAO-related derecognition, further understating its capital strength versus rivals. Citing an independent BAK Economics study forecasting up to CHF 34 billion in lost Swiss GDP over ten years from the foreign participation rules, UBS questioned the government’s impact assessment but stressed it will maintain its 2026 financial targets and planned capital returns, remain committed to its diversified business model, and seek ways to protect shareholders while limiting adverse effects on clients and employees as the parliamentary process unfolds.

More about ETRACS Alerian MLP Index ETN Class B

UBS Group AG, headquartered in Zurich and Basel, is a global financial services provider and Switzerland’s largest bank, with a core focus on wealth management, domestic Swiss banking, asset management and a targeted investment banking franchise. Following its acquisition of Credit Suisse, UBS operates as a systemically important institution with a substantial international footprint and is subject to some of the world’s most stringent capital regulations.

Buzbuzian is tasked with leading a nationwide rollout of the clinically validated alcohol metabolism and hangover-relief beverage, beginning with expansion into South Florida and Texas, and driving broader market penetration in the fast-growing functional beverage segment.

Unbuzzd, backed by a peer-reviewed clinical study demonstrating efficacy in speeding alcohol metabolism and reducing hangover symptoms, is targeting health-conscious consumers through a clean-label, ready-to-mix powder format and a multi-channel distribution strategy spanning pharmacies, liquor stores, supermarkets and e-commerce. For Quantum BioPharma, which holds nearly 20% of Unbuzzd Wellness and a significant royalty interest, the ramp-up of national distribution in a functional beverage market projected to reach $198 billion in 2026 represents a potentially meaningful upside to its revenue diversification and positions the group at the intersection of biotech innovation and consumer wellness.

More about Quantum Biopharma

Quantum BioPharma Ltd. is a biopharmaceutical company building a portfolio of innovative assets and biotech solutions targeting neurodegenerative and metabolic disorders and alcohol misuse. Through its subsidiary Lucid Psycheceuticals, it is developing Lucid-MS, a patented new chemical entity that has shown preclinical potential to prevent and reverse myelin degradation in multiple sclerosis models.

The company also invented unbuzzd, an over-the-counter beverage that accelerates alcohol metabolism and reduces hangover symptoms, and spun out its OTC version to Unbuzzd Wellness Inc. Quantum BioPharma retains a 19.84% stake in Unbuzzd Wellness as of December 31, 2025, along with a royalty stream on unbuzzd sales and full rights to develop related products for pharmaceutical and medical uses.

An update from Kustom Entertainment ( (KUST) ) is now available.

Effective April 22, 2026, Kustom Entertainment, Inc. implemented a 1-for-5 reverse stock split of its common stock and a proportional reduction in authorized share capital, after its board approved the move on April 5 and set April 7 as the record date. The transaction cut outstanding common shares from 2,633,063 to 526,613 and reduced authorized common shares from 66,666,666 to 13,333,333, with the stock beginning to trade on a split-adjusted basis on Nasdaq under a new CUSIP.

The reverse split is aimed at lifting Kustom Entertainment’s share price to satisfy Nasdaq’s minimum $1.00 bid requirement, a step that helps preserve the company’s listing and market visibility for investors. The action comes as the company continues to build out its live event production and proprietary ticketing operations, anchored by its flagship Country Stampede music festival in Kansas, underscoring a strategy to stabilize its capital markets profile while pursuing growth in the live events sector.

data.description:
Claim 30% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

BCE ( (TSE:BCE) ) has provided an update.

BCE Inc. reported on April 21, 2026 the results of shareholder elections to convert between its fixed-rate Series AG and floating-rate Series AH preferred shares, effective May 1, 2026. After these conversions, the company will have 9,375,684 Series AG shares and 2,832,114 Series AH shares outstanding, both continuing to trade on the Toronto Stock Exchange.

For the five-year period starting May 1, 2026, Series AG preferred shares will pay a fixed quarterly dividend at an annual rate of 5.30%, while Series AH will maintain a monthly floating dividend tied to the prime rate and an adjustment factor linked to market pricing. The announcement clarifies BCE’s future preferred share capital structure and dividend terms, providing income-oriented investors with transparent options between fixed and floating yield exposure.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSE:BCE) stock is a Buy with a C$44.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on BCE stock, see the TSE:BCE Stock Forecast page.
